rss.title: Gang-controlled streets, shuttered newsrooms: How violence is eroding Haiti’s media
rss.snippet: Journalists working in Haiti are under constant threat of death or injury from rapidly expanding criminal gangs, as they continue to report news and information which they hope will help keep fellow citizens safe.

rss.title: Attacks on media workers must end, UN urges
rss.snippet: War in the Middle East has made Lebanon the deadliest country for media workers so far this year, but practically no country offers a safe environment in which to be a journalist, according to the UN High Commissioner for Human Rights.

rss.title: World News in Brief: Syria human rights update, Cuba post-hurricane support still vital, impunity and violence in Myanmar
rss.snippet: Three mass graves were recently uncovered in northeastern Syria, including one reportedly at the site of a former detention centre run by the Kurdish-backed Syrian Armed Forces (SDF).

arxiv_papers.title: Peer Identity Bias in Multi-Agent LLM Evaluation: An Empirical Study Using the TRUST Democratic Discourse Analysis Pipeline
arxiv_papers.snippet: The TRUST democratic discourse analysis pipeline exposes its large language model (LLM) components to peer model identity through multiple structural channels -- a design feature whose bias implications have not previously been empirically tested. arxiv_papers.title: Trajectory-Aware Reliability Modeling of Democratic Systems
arxiv_papers.snippet: Failures in complex systems often emerge through gradual degradation and the propagation of stress across interacting components rather than through isolated shocks. Democratic systems exhibit similar dynamics, where weakening institutions can trigger cascading deterioration in related institutional

podcast: [#239 – Rose Hadshar on why automating all human labour will break our political system] The most important political question in the age of advanced AI might not be who wins elections.
